feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Integration
feed.subtitle: Cloud Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Base64 String Issues When Posting Attachments via ODataV2 API
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on uploading attachments to supplier invoices in SAP using the API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV via SAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration). While I successfully upload the file to SAP, I encounter an issue when attempting to open it—it appears to be corrupted. I suspect that the problem lies within the payload I am sending, specifically in how the file content is being handled.

Here is the JSON body payload I am using:

 

{
 "AttachmentContent":
  {
    "MimeType": "application/pdf",
    "Content": base64
 }
}

 



How I Generate the base64 File Content in Javascript:

 

async function fileToBase64(filePath) {
    return await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
       fs.readFile(filePath, (err, data) => {
           if (err) {
               reject(`Error reading file: ${err.message}`);
           } else {
               var base64 = data.toString('base64');
               resolve(base64);
           }
       });
   });
}

 


The upload to SAP completes without errors.
When attempting to open the file in SAP, it fails, indicating that the file is corrupted or invalid.

Since the OData receiver in CPI only accepts XML payloads, I use a JSON-to-XML converter. The generated XML looks like this:(deletet the base64 string because it is too long anyway)

 

<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?> 
<AttachmentContentSet> 
<AttachmentContent> 
<MimeType>application/pdf</MimeType> 
<Content>Base64String</Content> 
</AttachmentContent> 
</AttachmentContentSet>

 

 I would greatly appreciate guidance on this topic.

 Thank you for your help and insights!

entry.title: How to achieve sequencing in SOAP sender messages in SAP CPI.
entry.content:
Hello CPI Experts,
We are receiving messages from SAP S/4 via SOAP Sender adapter.. SOA set up is done in S/4 system.

It is SAP sender Business partner Service used for replicating the supplier and factories.

Issue is if we receive the same message at same time with same millisecond.Duplicate suppliers are created at target system..This is one issue.

Second is target system API doesnt supports multithreading.. So messages should process in Sequence.

Receiver is http call.How to achieve this in CPI?

Regards,

Karthiga

.

.

entry.title: Is there a way to make a JAR a global resource in CPI like a Script Collection for instance?
entry.content:
Hello community members,

This is the first time I ask a question here so hopefully I'll do that correctly 🙂

My issue is that I need to reuse Groovy classes, that I build for a special purpose, in many different packages containing custom TPM Groovy mappings.  To include the pre-compiled JAR in an IFlow as local ressource is not an issue - this works very well. Today I was searching for a way to make my first JAR available as a global ressource.
The only option that allowed me to upload the library was the CPI Script Collection but this seems to allow classless JARs, containing multiple scripts, only. When I tried to run my mapping with the Script Collection added the test ended with class not found exceptions.

Does anyone know of a way to achieve my request or do I have to wait until SAP offers a way to make JARs globally available in the CPI ecosystem?

Best regards,
Mathias

entry.title: Facing issue while Decoding Base64 Encoded PDF in CPI
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am trying to decode a Base64 Encoded pdf in CPI with the standard Base64 Decoder but it looks like the Decoder generating faulty PDF as after saving the content as .pdf it is not opening , showing document is damaged and can't open the file.

I tried to Decode the same Base64 string on Notepad++ and saved the decoded content as .pdf. The file is opening perfectly.

Even I tried replacing the Standard Decoder with Groovy but issue still persists, Please suggest how to resolve this.

 

Regards,

Arvik

entry.title: How to trigger IDOC based on status change on Material (integration question)
entry.content:
Hello,

I'd like to trigger an integration for Material sync in S4HANA cloud edition but only for cases when Material reaches status 71 or 72. When this happens, I'd like to send an IDOC to my integration platform.

Could you guys please let me know what are my options to do this a which option is the best? I believe I'd have to setup some sort of custom trigger in Material master?

Thank you!

Petr

entry.title: IAS/IPS: UPN in Azure and UserName in Cloud Apps
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

The following problem:

As far as I know, the UPN is typically set as an email address.

In my scenario, users are provisioned from Azure -> IAS -> cloud applications.

However, when provisioning users from IAS  (for example) to Enable Now, the usernames are created as email addresses.

 

 

How can I avoid this?

For example, in Entra ID Source System, the User Name is defined in the Default Code as follows:

{
"sourcePath": "$.userPrincipalName",
"targetPath": "$.userName",
"correlationAttribute": true
},

So, this means that the UPN (Azure) becomes the Login Name ( in IAS).

For example, if I map the employee ID to the username in the IPS transformation, the user is created in Enable Now with the employee ID (which would be ideal for us) as the username, but the users cannot authenticate in Azure with the employee ID.

Is there a way to change the UPN?

Or is there another solution to ensure that the usernames in cloud applications are not created as email addresses, while users can still authenticate in Azure?

Thank you very much !!!

Best regards

entry.title: [CPI-Groovy]: Sort XML Nodes based on the start date
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a requirement to sort the EmpJob nodes based on start dates only when there is sinlge EmpEmployment node and jobInfoNav has multiple EmpJob nodes which contains emplStatusNav/PicklistOption/localeLabel is Active in the ascending order. Eg. I am expecting EmpJob/startDate = 2024-11-01T00:00:00.000 in the first node and then EmpJob/startDate = 2025-01-01T00:00:00.000 in the second node. The payload contains multiple records, if these conditions are not satisfied there should not be any sorting done to nodes.

I have tried with the below-mentioned code but it is not working.

Any help or suggestions would be highly appreciated.

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
import groovy.xml.XmlUtil;
import groovy.util.NodeList
import groovy.util.XmlParser;
import groovy.xml.MarkupBuilder;
import java.util.Date;
import java.text.SimpleDateFormat;
import java.util.Calendar;

def Message processData(Message message) {
    def body = message.getBody(java.lang.String);
    def payload = new XmlParser().parseText(body);
    
        payload.PerPerson.each{rec ->  
        def countofEmpEmployment = rec.employmentNav.EmpEmployment.size()
        if (countofEmpEmployment <= 1){
            def countofEmpJob = rec.employmentNav.EmpEmployment.jobInfoNav.EmpJob.size();
            def EmpJobStatus = rec.employmentNav.EmpEmployment.jobInfoNav.EmpJob.emplStatusNav.PicklistOption.localeLabel.text()
            if(EmpJobStatus.contains("ActiveActive")){
            rec.value = rec.employmentNav.EmpEmployment.jobInfoNav.EmpJob.sort{sd ->
                    sd.startDate.text()}
            }
        }
    } 
    message.setBody(XmlUtil.serialize(payload));
    return message;
}

Regards,
Pavan

entry.title: Combining multiple IDOCs into one file at the receiving end in SAP CPI.
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have a requirement to combine multiple idocs into one single file. It is a DESADV.DELVRY03 structure. 

So the output structure should be like;

<DELVRY03>

<IDOC Begin="1">

</IDOC>

</DELVRY03>

For my requirement, it uses Trading Partner Management and in TPM only packet size of 1 supports. So before entering TPM I have used a splitter, and at the receiver end, I have used a custom integration flow and I need to combine the IDOCs using SAP_CorrelationID. 

How is it possible to do the above requirement in SAP CPI?

Thanks,

Radhika

entry.title: Regarding the sap systems and non-sap systems
entry.content:
Hi CPI FAMILY,

I AM VERY NEW CPI, COULD YOU PLEASE GIVE ME ABOUT THE WHICH ARE THE SAP AND NON-SAP systems we do use normally in IFLOWS.

Please describe the SAP&NON-SAP.

entry.title: Error when replicate Business Partner from S/4HANA Cloud Public to SAP Cloud for Customer (CPI)
entry.content:
Hello there,

I´m try to integrate SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public to SAP Cloud for Customer with SAP Integration Suite, but, when I´m try to replicate BP to C4C using the communication arrangement SAP_COM_0008, SAP C4C return the next error for integration:

No supported role for business partner 1xxxxxxx

Someone have any idea, how I can solve this issue?

 

Thanks,

entry.title: SAP API Management - Set Basic Authentication for API Proxy in Integration Suite
entry.content:
Dear All,

In SAP Integration Suite, Basic Authentication offers a simple way to safeguard your API proxies using a username and password. This blog covers how to configure Basic Authentication for your API proxy in API Management.

Follow the Below Steps to Set Basic Authentication to your API Proxy.

Step 1 : Create a Proxy with your target endpoint (For test purpose i used httpbin URL)

https://httpbin.org/get

 

 



Step 2 : Go to Policies and Assign Basic Authentication in PreFlow Incoming request Stream

 

 Provide the below code in the policy editor:

 

<BasicAuthentication async='true' continueOnError='false' enabled='true' xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
	<Operation>Decode</Operation>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
	<User ref='current.username'></User>
	<Password ref='current.password'></Password>
	<Source>request.header.Authorization</Source>
</BasicAuthentication>

 

 

Step 3 : Add Raise Fault Policy in PreFlow Incoming Stream (Next to the Basic Authentication Policy)

 

 

<RaiseFault async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <FaultResponse>
        <Set>
            <Headers/>
            <Payload contentType="application/json">{"status" : "Error", "messege" : "401 Unauthorized" } </Payload>
            <StatusCode>401</StatusCode>
            <ReasonPhrase>Unauthorized</ReasonPhrase>
        </Set>
    </FaultResponse>
    <IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
</RaiseFault>

 

In RaiseFault Policy ConditionString Provide below Code (Replace "YOUR_USERNAME" & "YOUR_PASSWORD" with your credentials)

 

(current.username != "YOUR_USERNAME")  OR (current.password != "YOUR_PASSWORD")

 

Save and Deploy.

Testing : 

To Test the Basic Authentication Try a GET Call for Your API Proxy in PostMan:

Positive Testing : 

Provide the exact Username and Password given in the Condition String :


Negative Testing : 

Provide an incorrect username and password to verify if access is correctly denied.

Incorrect Username :

Incorrect Password :

Thank You!

entry.title: CPI - Multiline html as String
entry.content:
EDIT: Cant seem to delete the post?
I figured out that multiline is indeed supported, so the issue must have been with some HTML that is not supported in the openPDF library. So I will debug there instead.

entry.title: SAP CPI open connection error with google ads
entry.content: Hi I am trying to connect with SAP CPI with Integration suite to google ads API. I have made all the the required steps in google developer console. But while creating instance i am getting error as "Failed to authenticate instance". Can somebody guide me what i am doing wrong ?

entry.title: How to read xml attached to a PDF file in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a requirement to read xml attached to a PDF file. Please let me know if same can be achieved somehow in SAP CPI.

Thanks in Advance!

Mayank

entry.title: SAP CPI open connection error "Failed to authenticate instance" with google ads
entry.content: I am trying to connect SAP CPI with google ADS but getting error as "Failed to authenticate instance".  Please let me know if somebody have any idea how to solve this error

entry.title: CPI Message Mapping source and target message
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

I am working on several iFlows for integrations between SAP Products (S4 MDM, Sales Cloud, CPQ) and a 3rd Party. I need to create message mappings. Right now I have got only simple tables with the names of the fields from the source and target system and how they should be mapped. Is my understanding right that: 
In order to do the message mapping I need source and target messages (XSD, XML, WSDL, Swagger/OpenAPI Spec JSON file) which have to be given to me by the sender and receiver party.

  1. Creating an XSD or OpenAPI Spec by myself my on the basis of the tables I got or even sample payloads might be tricky because it doesn't guarantee that the schemes I will get from it will be 100 % corresponding with what the sender/reciver will eventually send/expect (the developement at sender/receiver side is not finalized yet); the recation might be quite complicated as well.
  2. Would using another artifact instead of Message Mapping like e.g. XSLT Mapping be an option? I have no experience with XSLT so I am not sure if it offers the capabilieties required for the mapping (the mappings itself aren't going to be complicated: 1:1 field mapping, excluding fields or merging).
  3. The iFlows are to/from SAP Products, but we will be using some custom fields, so I can't use the APIs available in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub since those are provided only for standard solutions and data objects?

What are your thoughts on this issue?

Thanks in advance,

TG

entry.title: S4H to CPI. Need help
entry.content:
Hi friends. 

I'm following this blog (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-s4h-idoc-to-sap-cpi-inbound-idoc-interface/ba-p/13484455) and I'm getting stuck at step 6 of this blog. I'll include a picture to see if someone can help me figure out what am I doing wrong. 

When I try to add message type in the partner profile, the receiver port is not showing the CPI port. See screenshot attached. I have created port similar to how this blog shows in step 5. I'm assuming the port created in step 5 is what should be displayed in the receiver port in WE20, correct?

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration Error: HTTP header is larger than 8192 bytes.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

When processing a large payload in Cloud Integration with a S/4HANA Cloud target system using HTTP Adapter, I got the following error:

io.netty.handler.codec.TooLongFrameException: HTTP header is larger than 8192 bytes

 

I followed SAP Note 3146809(https://me.sap.com/notes/3146809/E) and tried the resolution(set "Cookie|x-csrf-token as Request Headers) , but the error still remained.

(P.s. I have also checked SAP Note 3537811 (https://me.sap.com/notes/3537811/E) but it does not really help because it merely asks me to reduce the size of HTTP header getting returned without letting me know how)

Does anyone know how to fix this?

===================================

As your reference, the following screenshots show the setting in my integration flow, which relate to http headers.

entry.title: CPI - Adapter RabbitMQ consumer Message - Wrong converstion to base64
entry.content:
I am using SAP CPI with a Groovy script, and when I post this message to the queue, it appears as Base64. However, in the logs of my middleware (SAP CPI), it is shown as a regular message.

When I post it to an online webhook, it also arrives normally as JSON.

Only in Adapter(AMQP)RabbitMQ does it arrive in Base64. The version is 4.0, and AMQP 1.0 is being used.

 

Message encode64 post in rabbit

eyJwYXR0ZXJuIjoidGVzdC5ldnQiLCJkYXRhIjp7IklEIjoiNjYzIiwiS2V5U0FQIjoiMDAwMSIsIlN0YXR1cyI6IlMiLCJNZW5zYWdlU0FQIjoiQ2xpZW50ZSAwMDAxIGNyZWF0ZWQgd2l0aCBzdWNjZXNzLiJ9fQ== 

Coding Groovy:

 

 

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

Message processData(Message message) { 
def fixedBody = '{"pattern":"test.evt","data":{"ID":"663","KeySAP":"0001","Status":"S","MensageSAP":"Cliente 0001 created with success."}}'

message.setBody(fixedBody)


message.setHeader("Content-Type", "text/plain")
message.setHeader("CamelHttpCharacterEncoding", "UTF-8")

return message 
}
